content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Nicole C. Wright 
Senior Deputy City Attorney 
Office of the City Attorney 
500 Castro Street 
PO Box 7540 
Mountain View, CA 94039-7540 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-033a 
Dear Ms. Wright: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
March 25, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for follow up advice regarding conflict of interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act §the "Act").1 You specifically asked for clarification of 
the analysis in the Wright Advice Letter, No. A-15-033 as it pertained lo City of Mountain View 
Bicycle and Pedestrian Advisory Committee Member Simon Purdon. 
You asked for further analysis on whether traffic impacts on Member Purdon's street for 
west bound traffic caused by the proposed road diet on California Street could result in a finding 
of materiality under Regulation 18702.2(a)(l0). Subdivision (a)(l0) provides that an effect is 
material if the decision: 
"Would clumge the character of the parcel of real property by 
s11bsta11tially altering traffic le,•els or i11te11sity of use, including parking, of 
property surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise 
levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the 
market value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial 
interest." (Emphasis added.) 
You noted in your correspondence of March 20, 2015, that while the existence of some 
potential traffic impact on Member Purdon' s street was identified in the City's preliminary 
analysis, that at this time it was unlikely the Study will propose removing a lane of westbound 
travel on California between Oak and Shoreline and even if they did the City cannot assess the 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references arc to the Go\emment Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A- l 5-033a 
Page No. 2 
severity of the potential changes or traffic levels at this time. Additional traffic information 
would be needed to provide an assessment since the Study is at a preliminary stage. 
Thus, the facts do not show that the current decisions will substantially alter traffic levels 
or intensity of use such that the decisions would change the character of the Member Purdon' s 
parcel. We reaffirm the conclusion in our letter Wright Advice Letter, No. A-15-033 that 
Member Purdon does not have a conflict of interest. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
..kiM- -z- J 
-
John W. Wallace 
¨ 
© 
Assistant General Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question: You asked for further analysis on whether traffic impacts on Member Purdon's street for west bound traffic caused by the proposed road diet on California Street could result in a finding of materiality under Regulation 18702.2(a)(10).
sections.conclusion: Thus, the facts do not show that the current decisions will substantially alter traffic levels or intensity of use such that the decisions would change the character of the Member Purdon's parcel. We reaffirm the conclusion in our letter Wright Advice Letter, No. A-15-033 that Member Purdon does not have a conflict of interest.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether traffic impacts on an official's street from a proposed road diet project would constitute a material conflict of interest under the materiality standard for real property effects.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirms that Member Purdon does not have a conflict of interest because the current preliminary analysis does not demonstrate that traffic impacts would substantially alter traffic levels or intensity of use to change the character of his property.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Follow-up advice letter referencing prior advice letter A-15-033. Clear question and conclusion sections extracted verbatim. Document appears complete with minor OCR artifacts in regulation citation formatting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked for further analysis on whether traffic impacts on Member Purdon's street for west bound traffic caused by the proposed road diet on California Street could result in a finding of materiality under Regulation 18702.2(a)(10).

CONCLUSION: Thus, the facts do not show that the current decisions will substantially alter traffic levels or intensity of use such that the decisions would change the character of the Member Purdon's parcel. We reaffirm the conclusion in our letter Wright Advice Letter, No. A-15-033 that Member Purdon does not have a conflict of interest.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides follow-up advice clarifying that preliminary traffic impact analysis does not show substantial alteration of traffic levels that would create a material conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.2(a)(10) for a city advisory committee member whose property would be affected by a proposed road diet project.
